claim.observation: istat of MFT entry 124753 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a non-resident $DATA stream of Actual Size 203196174 bytes (~194 MB). Recorded names include the 8.3 name 'WINDOW~1.ZZZ' and a long name composed of repeated 'Z' characters ending in a .ZZZ extension. Created 2018-09-05 13:21:53 (UTC), File Modified 2018-09-06 16:22:14 (UTC). icat of the first 16 data bytes returns all zeros.
claim.interpretation: Inode 124753 is a deleted ~194 MB file. Reading the start of its $DATA stream with icat returns zero bytes, so the content is not recoverable from these clusters. Its recorded name has been changed to a non-descriptive string of repeated 'Z' characters with a .ZZZ extension. fls reconstructs this same inode's path from the parent directory index as '...\research\Rare-Earth Elements\M&A Targets.zip', placing it as a sibling of the staged M&A research directory (see related finding). So a ~194 MB file associated by path with that M&A research directory was created 2018-09-05 and deleted with its content overwritten by 2018-09-06. The file's actual contents cannot be confirmed because the data is zeroed; this finding establishes only the existence, size, timing, deletion, name change, and content-overwrite of the file -- not the identity of any tool or actor responsible, nor that data left the host.

evidence_pointer.artifact: base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0), deleted file inode 124753 (fls path: Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets.zip)
evidence_pointer.command: istat -o 0 IMG 124753 | grep -E 'Not Allocated File|Actual Size|Created:|File Modified:|Name:' ; icat -o 0 IMG 124753 | head -c 16 | xxd
